_comment: CORRECTED 2026-07-08: prior version (get_technical_handoff_context, zero params) did not match the actual test — it couldn't carry a query to a specialist agent, so it could never prove agent-to-agent calling. This version defines the real candidate: a client-side function tool the Generic agent calls to hand a question to the Technical specialist. Schema shape (type:function, function.name/description/strict/parameters) copied verbatim from https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/algolia-ai/agent-studio/how-to/tools/client-side-tools.

function.name: consult_technical_specialist
function.description: Call this when the user's question requires deep React Spectrum implementation code or version-specific API details. Pass the user's full question verbatim as `query`. The caller (client) will get a real answer from the Technical specialist agent and return it as the tool result.

query.description: The user's question, verbatim, to hand to the Technical specialist agent.

_resume_pattern_source: https://www.algolia.com/doc/guides/algolia-ai/agent-studio/how-to/tools/mcp-tools documents the wire-level pause/resume pattern for MCP tool approvals; client-side 'function' tools are documented to use the same conceptual round-trip via the InstantSearch addToolResult() helper, but the docs do not spell out the raw HTTP body for that specific case. There is NO separate endpoint -- the caller re-POSTs to the SAME /completions endpoint with the full message history plus the new tool result field. This spike empirically determines the exact shape for the client_side (function) case, since only the MCP approval shape is documented verbatim.

_example_resume_request_shape_from_docs_._note: This is the MCP-approval shape, not the function-tool-result shape. Task 3/5/6 of this spike test candidate shapes empirically for the plain function-tool-result case (most likely a tool-result message part keyed by toolCallId, per the AI-SDK v4 convention already parsed client-side in web/src/lib/agentStudio.ts).
